note: jquery_ui_effects is a passive asset/library provider (no config UI, no permissions, no services, no .module). Its libraries are declared by the base jquery_ui module's hook_library_info_alter reading jquery_ui.libraries.data.json. All cases are `recipe` (response-graded). Facts are grounded in data.json, usage.md, and agent/api/jquery_ui_effects.md.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, why would I install the contrib `jquery_ui_effects` module? What does it provide?
evals.expected_output: It re-provides the jQuery UI Effects asset library as a contrib module after jQuery UI was deprecated and removed from Drupal core, so existing themes/modules keep working. It has no config, permissions or services.

evals.prompt: Does jquery_ui_effects work on its own, or does it need another module enabled?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the base jquery_ui module (jquery_ui:jquery_ui >=8.x-1.7). Both must be enabled; the base module actually registers the effects libraries.

evals.prompt: In a Drupal 11 render array, give the exact `#attached` line to load the jQuery UI fade effect.
What is the `jquery_ui_effects/core` library and when do I need it?
evals.expected_output: jquery_ui_effects/core is the effects engine (adds .effect(), effect-aware .show()/.hide()/.toggle(), easings and color animation). Every individual effect depends on it, so attaching a specific effect pulls it in automatically.

evals.prompt: Which individual jQuery UI effect sub-libraries can I attach with this module? Name several.
evals.expected_output: blind, bounce, clip, drop, explode, fade, fold, highlight, puff, pulsate, scale, shake, size, slide and transfer — each as jquery_ui_effects/<name>, each depending on jquery_ui_effects/core.

evals.prompt: In my own module's *.libraries.yml, how do I make my JS depend on the jQuery UI slide effect? Show the YAML.
evals.expected_output: Under my library's `dependencies:` list `- jquery_ui_effects/slide` (it pulls in jquery_ui_effects/core automatically).

evals.prompt: Where is the configuration/settings page for jquery_ui_effects, and what permissions does it add?
evals.expected_output: There is none: jquery_ui_effects has no configuration UI, no permissions and no services. You simply install it and attach the effect library ids.

evals.prompt: The jquery_ui_effects module has no jquery_ui_effects.libraries.yml. How do its libraries get declared?
evals.expected_output: The base jquery_ui module declares them: its jquery_ui_library_info_alter() reads jquery_ui.libraries.data.json and registers entries under the jquery_ui_effects namespace.
